note on sources: Ps 140 second set from MCI. Rest from RD. Lytia omitted.
ps140: Martyr and athlete, Hermolaus,* once you hid yourself in fear from the evil ones.* You rejoiced to reveal the hidden mystery of devotion and understanding* to the passion-bearer Panteleimon.* Then you revealed yourself and zealously died for Christ* who was well-pleased to suffer for us in his goodness.

Martyr and athlete, Hermolaus,* you desired a holy life in sanctity.* You became a bishop of the almighty Jesus,* and by your life-bearing sermons and grace* you saved the people from the darkness of impurity.* You have passed over to the heavens, washed in the blood of your suffering,* blessed one of God!

Let us praise in faith with holy words the martyrs of the Lord,* Hermippus, Hermolaus and Hermocrates!* They suffered in a wonderful way, killing the evil one* and washing themselves in the water of baptism!* They were not defiled again with other impurities.* Therefore let us keep the feast* and celebrate their memory!

O wondrous marvel!* As your fought the good fight, O holy Parasceve,* your courageously overcame* the arrogance of Antoninus.* You merited the crown and prize of your heavenly calling;* remember your servants who venerate you with faith,* as your stand before the Lord,* that he take pity on us.

Loving the Lord with your whole soul,* O illustrious martyr of Christ,* you patiently endured the punishing blows.* Torn by the scourge, undergoing all torments for the sake of your Beloved,* you received the merited crown.* Thus we acclaim you as blessed before God.

Venerable and famed martyr,* who could speak of your sufferings* and your courageous miracles in your struggles,* the patience and meekness you manifested,* as though you were one of the bodiless powers?* Thus, you became a companion of the angels in heaven,* O Parasceve, bride of the King and holy vessel of the Spirit.

O most glorious Parasceve, victorious virgin martyr,* through your mediations before God,* pray that, those who celebrate your suffering as is meet* may be delivered from temptations, and tribulations* and the judgment which is to come,* O most hymned martyr.
